basics.name: Koray Devecioglu
basics.label: Software engineer
basics.summary: Software engineer with a decade of experience across embedded systems, networking, and connected products. Currently building smart lighting experiences for Philips Hue at Signify; previously worked on embedded Wi-Fi at Airties, tactical and consumer networking at NETAŞ, and image processing at EVSTEK. Comfortable from kernel-level C up to product-shaped problems.

work.name: Signify
highlights: Build reliable, scalable smart lighting experiences for Philips Hue, focused on connected-system interaction and reliability.
Work across cross-functional teams to integrate intelligence into everyday environments and improve product experience.
Apply systems and problem-solving skills to performance and reliability challenges in smart lighting technology.

work.name: Airties
highlights: Led design and development of embedded software in C for Airties' Smart Wi-Fi portfolio.
Specialised in Linux kernel and IEEE 802.11; ran delivery on Agile cycles.
Tailored software features for major operator clients including AT&T and Vodafone.

work.name: NETAŞ
work.summary: Four and a half years of embedded networking work across three projects: TASMUS tactical field communications, the ZTE Home Gateway technology transfer, and Electric Vehicle charging.
highlights: Built embedded software in C/C++ for TASMUS, the Turkish Armed Forces' Tactical Field Communication System — routing architectures plus OSPF, BGP, PIM-SM, MSDP, IGMP, DHCP, ECMP, and load-balancing across TCP/IP and IPv4/IPv6.

Joined a technology-transfer project between ZTE (China) and NETAŞ (Turkey), including a one-month on-site stint with ZTE R&D in China; built modem web interfaces using TR-069, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and Lua.
Designed Dynamic Load Management algorithms for Electric Vehicle charging in C++ with a RAD Studio C++ Builder GUI; implemented DHCP, IPSec, and ZeroMQ networking layers.

work.name: EVSTEK Information Tech
highlights: Designed and implemented digital image-processing methods in C++.
Modelled and simulated project features in MATLAB and Simulink against spec.
